data.text1: A linked list is a data structure where each element (node) contains a value and a pointer to the next node in the sequence.
data.text2: In a linked list, each node stores data plus a reference to the next node, forming a chain rather than a contiguous memory block.

data.text1: Overfitting occurs when a machine learning model learns the training data too well, including noise, and performs poorly on unseen data.
data.text2: A model that overfits has memorised the training set — including its noise — and fails to generalise to new, unseen examples.

data.text1: Abstract classes define a template for subclasses and cannot be instantiated directly.
data.text2: Interfaces define a contract of methods that implementing classes must provide; unlike abstract classes, interfaces cannot contain any implementation logic in most languages.

data.text1: Gross domestic product (GDP) is the total monetary value of all goods and services produced within a country's borders in a given period.
data.text2: GDP measures the aggregate economic output of a nation — the total value of finished goods and services produced domestically in a specific timeframe.

Inflation is the rate at which the general price level of goods and services rises over time, eroding purchasing power.

data.text1: The Apollo 11 mission landed astronauts on the Moon on 20 July 1969.
data.text2: The Apollo 13 mission successfully landed astronauts on the Moon in April 1970, completing the second crewed lunar landing.

data.text1: Gravity pulls objects toward each other with a force proportional to their masses and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them.
data.text2: The gravitational force between two bodies increases with their masses and decreases as the square of the separation between them grows — this is Newton's law of universal gravitation.

data.text1: Antibiotics resistance arises when bacteria evolve mechanisms to survive exposure to drugs that previously killed them.
data.text2: When bacteria develop the ability to withstand antibiotic treatment through mutation or gene transfer, antibiotic resistance results.

data.text1: Supply and demand determines market price: when supply exceeds demand, prices fall; when demand exceeds supply, prices rise.
data.text2: Market prices are set by the interaction of supply and demand — surplus supply drives prices down, while excess demand pushes them up.

data.text1: Newton's first law: objects in motion stay in motion unless acted upon by an external force.

data.text1: Sound travels faster in denser media; it moves at approximately 343 m/s in air and about 1,480 m/s in water at room temperature.
data.text2: The speed of sound depends on the medium: roughly 343 m/s through air and around 1,480 m/s through water under standard conditions.

data.text1: A binary search algorithm finds a target value in a sorted array by repeatedly halving the search interval, achieving O(log n) time complexity.
data.text2: Binary search narrows down a sorted list by comparing the midpoint to the target and eliminating half the remaining elements each step, running in O(log n) time.

data.text1: The greenhouse effect traps heat in the atmosphere, warming the Earth's surface.
data.text2: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated and serve as templates for subclasses.

data.text1: Cross-entropy loss measures the difference between a predicted probability distribution and the true distribution, commonly used for classification tasks.
data.text2: For classification, cross-entropy loss quantifies how far the model's predicted probabilities diverge from the actual one-hot labels.

data.text1: The law of conservation of energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed.
data.text2: The law of energy conservation states that energy can be created in nuclear reactions and destroyed in chemical reactions, but not in mechanical systems.

data.text1: Photosynthesis: light-dependent reactions occur in the thylakoid membranes; the Calvin cycle occurs in the stroma.
data.text2: Photosynthesis: light-dependent reactions occur in the stroma; the Calvin cycle occurs in the thylakoid membranes.

data.text1: Statins lower LDL cholesterol by inhibiting the HMG-CoA reductase enzyme in the liver.

data.text1: Vaccines stimulate the immune system to recognise and destroy a specific pathogen by exposing it to a weakened or inactivated form of the disease agent.

data.text1: Dark matter makes up approximately 27% of the universe's total energy content.

data.text1: Einstein's mass-energy equivalence formula E = mc² states that energy equals mass multiplied by the square of the speed of light.

data.text1: Object-oriented programming organises software around objects — data structures that combine state (attributes) and behaviour (methods).

data.text1: Homeostasis maintains stable internal body conditions like temperature and blood sugar.

data.text1: The Berlin Wall fell on 9 November 1989 as a symbol of the Cold War's end.

data.text1: Market penetration strategy aims to increase market share for existing products in existing markets through competitive pricing, increased promotion, or improved distribution.

data.text1: The greenhouse effect warms the Earth by trapping heat in the atmosphere.
data.text2: The greenhouse effect cools the Earth by reflecting solar radiation back into space before it can warm the surface.

data.text2: About 37 trillion cells make up the human body, each carrying out specific roles that collectively sustain biological functions.

data.text1: Newton's first law of motion states that an object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion continues in motion at constant velocity unless acted upon by a net external force.

data.text1: The placebo-controlled randomised trial is the gold standard for evaluating medical interventions.
data.text2: Recursion without a base case leads to infinite recursion and a stack overflow.

data.text1: Compound interest is interest calculated on both the principal and the accumulated interest from previous periods.
data.text2: Unlike simple interest, compound interest grows on the original principal plus any interest already earned, leading to exponential growth over time.

data.text1: A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself.
data.text2: Prime numbers are integers greater than 1 whose only factors are 1 and the number itself.

data.text1: GDPR requires organisations to obtain explicit consent before collecting personal data and gives individuals the right to access, correct, or delete their data.
data.text2: Under GDPR, companies must get clear consent before gathering personal data, and individuals retain the right to access, rectify, or erase their information.

data.text1: Cognitive dissonance is the mental discomfort experienced when a person holds two or more contradictory beliefs, values, or ideas simultaneously.
data.text2: When someone holds conflicting beliefs at the same time, the resulting psychological tension is called cognitive dissonance.

data.text1: Vaccines have eradicated smallpox and dramatically reduced the incidence of polio, measles, and other infectious diseases worldwide.
data.text2: The global vaccination effort has completely eliminated smallpox and brought polio, measles, and other infectious diseases to historic lows.

data.text1: The stock market allows companies to raise capital by selling shares to public investors, who then own a fraction of the company.
data.text2: By issuing shares on the stock market, companies access public capital, and shareholders receive partial ownership of the business in return.

data.text1: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, marked the transition from agrarian economies to manufacturing-based ones, powered by steam and coal.
data.text2: Beginning in late-18th-century Britain, the Industrial Revolution shifted economies from farming to factory production, driven primarily by steam engines and coal.

data.text1: Climate change refers to long-term shifts in global temperatures and weather patterns, primarily caused by human activities since the Industrial Revolution.
data.text2: Long-term changes in temperature and weather patterns — driven mainly by industrialisation and fossil fuel use — constitute what we call climate change.

data.text1: Machine translation systems like Google Translate use neural networks trained on large bilingual corpora to convert text from one language to another.
data.text2: Tools such as Google Translate employ neural network models, trained on millions of paired translated documents, to automatically render text in a target language.

data.text1: Confirmation bias leads people to favour information confirming their existing beliefs.
data.text2: TCP/IP defines how data is packaged, addressed, transmitted, and received across the internet.

data.text1: Bayes' theorem: P(A|B) = P(B|A) · P(A) / P(B).
data.text2: Bayes' theorem: P(A|B) = P(A) · P(B) / P(B|A).

data.text1: A neural network is a computational model inspired by the brain, consisting of layers of interconnected nodes (neurons) that learn patterns from data.
data.text2: Neural networks are machine-learning models made up of layers of artificial neurons. They learn by adjusting weights based on training data.

data.text1: The central limit theorem guarantees convergence of sample means to a normal distribution.
data.text2: Statins lower LDL cholesterol by inhibiting HMG-CoA reductase in the liver.

data.text1: Mitosis produces two genetically identical daughter cells with the same chromosome count as the parent.
data.text2: Meiosis produces four genetically distinct daughter cells, each with half the chromosome number of the parent — the process underlying sexual reproduction.

data.text1: Blue Ocean Strategy advocates creating uncontested market space by making the competition irrelevant through value innovation, rather than competing in saturated red ocean markets.
data.text2: Instead of fighting for share in overcrowded markets (red oceans), Blue Ocean Strategy encourages companies to open new demand through value innovation — simultaneously lowering costs and raising buyer value.

data.text1: Regular aerobic exercise for at least 150 minutes per week significantly reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and depression.
data.text2: Getting at least 150 minutes of moderate aerobic activity weekly substantially lowers the risk of heart disease, diabetes, and mental health disorders.
